title: OpenTelemetry Collector Dropped Spans, Metrics, and Log Records — Pipeline Data-Loss Forensics
dataSources: OpenTelemetry Collector internal Prometheus exposition on /metrics ingested into mstats-compatible metric indexes (for example otel_metrics) with families otelcol_exporter_send_failed_*, otelcol_exporter_enqueue_failed_*, otelcol_processor_dropped_*, otelcol_processor_refused_*, otelcol_processor_batch_batch_send_size, otelcol_processor_filter_filtered_*, otelcol_receiver_accepted_*, otelcol_exporter_sent_*; structured collector logs sourcetype=otel:collector; optional Splunk HTTP Event Collector landing patterns (splunk_httpinput, http_event_collector, or services collector source paths) as arrival corridor checks; dead-letter or export failure archives modeled as sourcetype=otel:deadletter; Splunk CIM Performance summaries via tstats for underlying hosts; lookup otel_collector_inventory.csv keyed on collector_id for replica and endpoint set cross-checks.
app: OpenTelemetry Collector (opentelemetry-collector-contrib), Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector, Prometheus scrape or OpenTelemetry Prometheus receiver for /metrics, optional Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector add-on packaging, Splunk HTTP Event Collector endpoints for telemetry landing
spl:
`comment("UC-13.3.14 OTel pipeline data-loss forensics per exporter processor join inventory tstats perf earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats latest(_value) AS mv WHERE index=otel_metrics metric_name IN ("otelcol_exporter_send_failed_spans","otelcol_exporter_send_failed_metric_points","otelcol_exporter_send_failed_log_records","otelcol_exporter_enqueue_failed_spans","otelcol_exporter_enqueue_failed_metric_points","otelcol_exporter_enqueue_failed_log_records","otelcol_processor_dropped_spans","otelcol_processor_dropped_metric_points","otelcol_processor_dropped_log_records","otelcol_processor_refused_spans","otelcol_processor_refused_metric_points","otelcol_processor_refused_log_records","otelcol_processor_batch_batch_send_size","otelcol_processor_filter_filtered_spans","otelcol_processor_filter_filtered_metric_points","otelcol_processor_filter_filtered_log_records","otelcol_receiver_accepted_spans","otelcol_receiver_accepted_metric_points","otelcol_receiver_accepted_log_records","otelcol_exporter_sent_spans","otelcol_exporter_sent_metric_points","otelcol_exporter_sent_log_records") span=1m BY metric_name service_instance_id exporter processor
      | eval collector_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_instance_id,"otel_unk"))))
      | eval signal_family=case(match(metric_name,"log_record$"),"logs",match(metric_name,"metric_point"),"metrics",match(metric_name,"span$"),"traces",match(metric_name,"batch"),"batch",match(metric_name,"filter"),"filter",true(),"mixed")
      | eval lane="mstats_loss"
      | eval exporter_tag=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(exporter,"__none__"))))
      | fields _time collector_id signal_family metric_name mv exporter_tag lane ]
    [ search (index=otel OR index=observability_logs OR index=containers) sourcetype=otel:collector earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval r=lower(_raw)
      | where match(r,"send failed|enqueue failed|dropped|refused|filtered|429|5\\d\\d|handshake|partial success|rejected|resource exhausted|decision_cache|sender_queue|feature gate|schema")
      | eval collector_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(collector_id,service_instance_id,pod,host,"otel_log"))))
      | eval signal_family="mixed"
      | eval metric_name="collector_log_loss_hint"
      | eval mv=1.0
      | eval lane="otel_logs"
      | eval exporter_tag="logline"
      | fields _time collector_id signal_family metric_name mv exporter_tag lane ]
    [ search (index=main OR index=otel_sink OR index=observability) (sourcetype=splunk_httpinput OR sourcetype=http_event_collector OR source=*services*collector*) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval collector_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,"hec_lane"))))
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats count AS mv BY _time collector_id
      | eval signal_family="sink"
      | eval metric_name="hec_arrival_proxy"
      | eval lane="hec_corridor"
      | eval exporter_tag="hec"
      | fields _time collector_id signal_family metric_name mv exporter_tag lane ]
    [ search (index=otel OR index=observability OR index=main) sourcetype=otel:deadletter earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval collector_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(collector_id,host,pod,"dl_unk"))))
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats count AS mv BY _time collector_id
      | eval signal_family="deadletter"
      | eval metric_name="deadletter_record"
      | eval lane="deadletter_file"
      | eval exporter_tag="dl"
      | fields _time collector_id signal_family metric_name mv exporter_tag lane ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_ld latest(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_us FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
      | eval collector_id=lower(trim(Performance.host))
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval signal_family="host"
      | eval metric_name="perf_dm_anchor"
      | eval mv=coalesce(mem_us,0)
      | eval lane="tstats_perf"
      | eval exporter_tag="perf"
      | fields _time collector_id signal_family metric_name mv exporter_tag lane cpu_ld mem_us ]
| eval collector_id=coalesce(collector_id,"unknown_coll")
| eval exporter_tag=coalesce(exporter_tag,"__na__")
| bucket _time span=1m
| eval v_acc=if(match(metric_name,"receiver_accepted"),mv,0)
| eval v_sent=if(match(metric_name,"exporter_sent"),mv,0)
| eval v_sfail=if(match(metric_name,"send_failed"),mv,0)
| eval v_eqfail=if(match(metric_name,"enqueue_failed"),mv,0)
| eval v_pdrop=if(match(metric_name,"processor_dropped"),mv,0)
| eval v_pref=if(match(metric_name,"processor_refused"),mv,0)
| eval v_filt=if(match(metric_name,"filter_filtered"),mv,0)
| stats sum(v_acc) AS acc_sum sum(v_sent) AS sent_sum sum(v_sfail) AS sfail_sum sum(v_eqfail) AS eqfail_sum sum(v_pdrop) AS pdrop_sum sum(v_pref) AS pref_sum sum(v_filt) AS filt_sum max(eval(if(match(metric_name,"batch_batch_send"),mv,null()))) AS batch_span_hint max(cpu_ld) AS cpu_ld max(mem_us) AS mem_us values(lane) AS lanes BY _time collector_id exporter_tag
| sort 0 collector_id exporter_tag - _time
| streamstats window=5 global=f current=t first(acc_sum) AS a0 last(acc_sum) AS a1 first(sent_sum) AS s0 last(sent_sum) AS s1 first(sfail_sum) AS f0 last(sfail_sum) AS f1 BY collector_id exporter_tag
| eval acc_delta=round(a1-a0,4)
| eval sent_delta=round(s1-s0,4)
| eval sfail_delta=round(f1-f0,4)
| eval gap_ratio_pct=if(acc_delta>0.001, round(100*(acc_delta-sent_delta)/acc_delta,3), null())
| eventstats median(sfail_delta) AS med_sfail_delta BY collector_id exporter_tag
| eval severity=case(sfail_delta>10 OR (isnotnull(gap_ratio_pct) AND gap_ratio_pct>5),"critical", sfail_delta>0 OR eqfail_sum>0 OR (isnotnull(gap_ratio_pct) AND gap_ratio_pct>1) OR pdrop_sum>0 OR pref_sum>0 OR filt_sum>0,"high", true(),"low")
| eval loss_focus=case(sfail_delta>0 AND eqfail_sum>0,"exporter_retry_queue", sfail_delta>0,"exporter_hard_fail", pdrop_sum>0,"processor_drop", pref_sum>0,"processor_refuse", filt_sum>0,"filter_intent", true(),"observe")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","isolate_exporter_validate_tls_tokens_throttle_and_queue_caps", severity=="high","diff_config_replicas_audit_processors_feature_gates", true(),"baseline_review_after_change_window")
| join type=left max=0 collector_id
    [| inputlookup otel_collector_inventory.csv
     | fields collector_id env owner_team role image_tag otlp_endpoint_set ]
| eval env=coalesce(env,"unknown_env")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval otlp_endpoint_set=coalesce(otlp_endpoint_set,"unset_endpoints")
| table _time collector_id env owner_team exporter_tag otlp_endpoint_set lanes acc_delta sent_delta gap_ratio_pct sfail_delta pdrop_sum pref_sum filt_sum severity loss_focus recommended_action cpu_ld mem_us
description: Forensically attributes OpenTelemetry Collector data loss across traces, metrics, and logs by correlating internal /metrics counters for exporter send and enqueue failures, processor drops and refuses, filter removals, batch send sizing, and accepted-versus-sent deltas per exporter label, enriched with collector logs, dead-letter lanes, HEC corridor volume, Performance host anchors, and otel_collector_inventory.csv. The angle is pinpointing which pipeline stage and which exporter replica loses telemetry, including replica drift and schema or feature-gate mismatch, rather than overall throughput headroom (UC-13.3.15), CPU or memory saturation (UC-13.3.16), sampling policy design (UC-13.3.7), Prometheus scrape health (UC-13.3.2 and UC-13.3.11), or inventory coverage gaps (UC-13.3.4).
value: Reliability leaders gain defensible evidence about silent telemetry holes before customer dashboards look healthy while traces or metrics are incomplete: exporter failures, processor-stage drops, and configuration skew become actionable owner assignments instead of guesswork, preserving trust in observability budgets and incident timelines.
implementation: Land OpenTelemetry Collector internal Prometheus metrics into an mstats index such as otel_metrics, forward sourcetype=otel:collector and otel:deadletter lanes plus a coarse HEC corridor pattern, publish otel_collector_inventory.csv keyed to service_instance labels, accelerate Splunk CIM Performance for collector hosts, save observability_uc_13_3_14_otel_loss_forensics every five minutes with earliest=-4h@h, and page on critical or high severity after deduping collector_id and exporter_tag pairs.
visualization: Line charts for accepted versus sent deltas and send_failed rates, bar charts for loss_focus counts by exporter_tag, tables with inventory joins, and pipeline diff panels comparing otel_collector_inventory.csv otlp_endpoint_set against live metric label sets.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries on hosts and pods that run collector workloads; retain at least thirty days of summaries for quarterly attestations. Pair with Change summaries or change-ticket metadata when correlating feature gate or image rollouts with per-replica processor metric drift.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector

knownFalsePositives: Rolling upgrades can reorder DaemonSets so a minority of replicas temporarily run alternate YAML while metrics cardinality shifts; wait for full rollout before paging unless send_failed persists past stabilization. Intentional filter processors raise otelcol_processor_filter_filtered_* during policy changes; join change calendars and filter names before treating filters as incidents. Lab chaos tests that inject 429 responses will trip this control on purpose; tag those windows in a suppression lookup. Scrape loss of the internal metrics endpoint mimics innocence while logs still show pain; monitor scrape targets independently. Some distributions omit otelcol_exporter_sent_* for certain exporters, producing null gap ratios without proving health; rely on send_failed, enqueue_failed, and dead-letter lanes in those builds. Dead-letter sourcetype misconfiguration can under-count failures when file rotation clears evidence quickly; validate retention and permissions. HEC corridor counts are coarse aggregates and may move for reasons unrelated to collectors, such as unrelated applications sharing the same token; treat that arm as corroboration, not sole proof. Partial backend outages may heal mid-window, creating short spikes that clear automatically; require dwell time aligned to error budget policy before waking executives.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability engineers who operate OpenTelemetry Collector agent and gateway fleets, site reliability engineers who interpret customer-facing gaps in traces and metrics, and the teams that manage Splunk HTTP Event Collector tokens, downstream observability backends, and GitOps pipelines that render collector configuration per cluster. This use case isolates forensic attribution of true data loss inside the collector: counters that prove exporters failed to hand off spans, metric points, or log records, processors that dropped or refused items after admission, enqueue failures that signal retry queues could not accept work, and filter processors that intentionally remove signal. It is deliberately not UC-13.3.15, which centers sustained pipeline throughput, exporter queue fill, and memory limiter pressure as early saturation warnings. It is not UC-13.3.16, which centers CPU and RAM saturation on collector pods and hosts. It is not UC-13.3.7, which centers sampling policy coverage and statistical representativeness rather than mechanical drops after admission. It is not UC-13.3.2, which remains the home for Prometheus scrape and federation integrity. It is not UC-13.3.11, which remains the home for Prometheus target scrape failures. It is not UC-13.3.4, which remains the home for inventory versus emitter reconciliation.

Operational collectors expose internal Prometheus metrics on the telemetry service, commonly scraped from port 8888 at path /metrics. Those series name the components that accepted, transformed, filtered, batched, and exported telemetry. When you compare otelcol_receiver_accepted_* to otelcol_exporter_sent_* within the same service_instance_id and pipeline, a sustained gap that is not explained by intentional sampling points toward exporter serialization failures, partial success handling, or backend throttling. When otelcol_exporter_send_failed_* or otelcol_exporter_enqueue_failed_* rise in the same window, you have hard evidence of loss or high risk of loss at the exporter boundary. When otelcol_processor_dropped_* or otelcol_processor_refused_* rise while exporter failures stay quiet, the failure is earlier in the graph, often batch sizing, memory limiting, tail sampling decision caches, or attribute transforms that reject malformed data.

Replica drift is a first-class signal. If otelcol_processor_* counters appear only for a subset of service_instance_id values while configuration Git claims homogeneity, you may have a partial rollout, a feature gate mismatch, or a build tag skew between DaemonSet revisions. Cross-check otel_collector_inventory.csv, which should list every expected collector_id, environment, owner team, role, image tag, and declared OTLP endpoint set, against live internal metrics labels. Schema and feature gate mismatches often surface as receiver refuses or processor refuses long before operators notice missing charts, especially after flipping experimental stabilizations.

RBAC and secret handling follow the same discipline as other platform controls. Read-only automation accounts may search metrics and logs without administrative cluster roles. Store tokens in vault-backed secrets with overlap during rotation windows so exporter failures are not misread as application outages. Network policy must still allow scrape paths for internal metrics from approved observability namespaces while blocking lateral movement. Legal and privacy reviewers should confirm dead-letter files and diagnostic logs do not retain regulated payloads longer than policy allows.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A lands OpenTelemetry Collector internal metrics into an mstats-compatible index such as otel_metrics. Enable the collector telemetry stanza so Prometheus exposition includes otelcol_exporter_send_failed_spans, otelcol_exporter_send_failed_metric_points, otelcol_exporter_send_failed_log_records, otelcol_exporter_enqueue_failed_spans, otelcol_exporter_enqueue_failed_metric_points, otelcol_exporter_enqueue_failed_log_records, otelcol_processor_dropped_*, otelcol_processor_refused_*, otelcol_processor_batch_batch_send_size, otelcol_processor_filter_filtered_*, otelcol_receiver_accepted_*, and otelcol_exporter_sent_* when your build exports them. Preserve service_instance_id, exporter, processor, and pipeline labels when present. If your distribution renames metrics, add FIELDALIAS rules before production rollout.

Lane B forwards Splunk Distribution or contrib collector logs with sourcetype=otel:collector into observability or containers indexes. Tune line breaking for multiline stack traces and keep JSON parsing strict enough to extract exporter names and error codes. Gateway and agent pods should share predictable sourcetype naming so multisearch arms do not miss gateway-only failures.

Lane C provides a coarse Splunk HTTP Event Collector corridor signal: landing indexes that receive telemetry through HTTP inputs, using sourcetype patterns such as splunk_httpinput or http_event_collector or sources that reference services collector paths. This arm is not a perfect per-span ledger; it is a cross-check that bytes still arrive when internal metrics imply exporter success. Rename indexes and sourcetypes to match your deployment.

Lane D captures dead-letter or export failure archives, modeled here as sourcetype=otel:deadletter, which may be implemented via filelog exporter outputs or dedicated failure buses. If you use a different sourcetype, align the search arm accordingly.

Lane E queries Splunk CIM Performance summaries with tstats for hosts and pods that run collectors, joining later on normalized collector_id keys. Accelerate Performance summaries on hosts that map collector infrastructure; retain enough history for quarterly audits.

Maintain otel_collector_inventory.csv with columns collector_id, env, owner_team, role, image_tag, and otlp_endpoint_set. Refresh from GitOps and image inventories weekly. The saved search joins this lookup to explain who must act when only one replica shows processor drops.

Example curl helpers from an authorized bastion for design reviews: curl -fsS http://otel-collector.observability.svc.cluster.local:8888/metrics | rg otelcol_exporter_send_failed ; kubectl -n observability logs deploy/otel-collector-gateway --tail=200 | rg -i "send failed|enqueue|refused".

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_3_14_otel_loss_forensics with a five-minute schedule during steady production, dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate collector_id and exporter_tag pairs for fifteen minutes unless severity escalates. Include loss_focus, gap_ratio_pct, and recommended_action in pager narratives so incident commanders open exporter runbooks before muting.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans internal metrics for exporter failures, enqueue failures, processor drops and refuses, filter removals, accepted versus sent ratios, and batch sizing; structured collector logs; HEC corridor volume; dead-letter volume; and Performance host anchors. streamstats estimates short-window deltas for accepted and sent counters per collector_id and exporter_tag. eventstats adds median failure context for baseline hints. case encodes severity using exporter failure deltas, gap ratios, enqueue failures, and processor-stage signals. coalesce backfills inventory fields after the join subsearch that wraps inputlookup otel_collector_inventory.csv.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.3.14 OTel pipeline data-loss forensics per exporter processor join inventory tstats perf earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats latest(_value) AS mv WHERE index=otel_metrics metric_name IN ("otelcol_exporter_send_failed_spans","otelcol_exporter_send_failed_metric_points","otelcol_exporter_send_failed_log_records","otelcol_exporter_enqueue_failed_spans","otelcol_exporter_enqueue_failed_metric_points","otelcol_exporter_enqueue_failed_log_records","otelcol_processor_dropped_spans","otelcol_processor_dropped_metric_points","otelcol_processor_dropped_log_records","otelcol_processor_refused_spans","otelcol_processor_refused_metric_points","otelcol_processor_refused_log_records","otelcol_processor_batch_batch_send_size","otelcol_processor_filter_filtered_spans","otelcol_processor_filter_filtered_metric_points","otelcol_processor_filter_filtered_log_records","otelcol_receiver_accepted_spans","otelcol_receiver_accepted_metric_points","otelcol_receiver_accepted_log_records","otelcol_exporter_sent_spans","otelcol_exporter_sent_metric_points","otelcol_exporter_sent_log_records") span=1m BY metric_name service_instance_id exporter processor
      | eval collector_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_instance_id,"otel_unk"))))
      | eval signal_family=case(match(metric_name,"log_record$"),"logs",match(metric_name,"metric_point"),"metrics",match(metric_name,"span$"),"traces",match(metric_name,"batch"),"batch",match(metric_name,"filter"),"filter",true(),"mixed")
      | eval lane="mstats_loss"
      | eval exporter_tag=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(exporter,"__none__"))))
      | fields _time collector_id signal_family metric_name mv exporter_tag lane ]
    [ search (index=otel OR index=observability_logs OR index=containers) sourcetype=otel:collector earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval r=lower(_raw)
      | where match(r,"send failed|enqueue failed|dropped|refused|filtered|429|5\\d\\d|handshake|partial success|rejected|resource exhausted|decision_cache|sender_queue|feature gate|schema")
      | eval collector_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(collector_id,service_instance_id,pod,host,"otel_log"))))
      | eval signal_family="mixed"
      | eval metric_name="collector_log_loss_hint"
      | eval mv=1.0
      | eval lane="otel_logs"
      | eval exporter_tag="logline"
      | fields _time collector_id signal_family metric_name mv exporter_tag lane ]
    [ search (index=main OR index=otel_sink OR index=observability) (sourcetype=splunk_httpinput OR sourcetype=http_event_collector OR source=*services*collector*) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval collector_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,"hec_lane"))))
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats count AS mv BY _time collector_id
      | eval signal_family="sink"
      | eval metric_name="hec_arrival_proxy"
      | eval lane="hec_corridor"
      | eval exporter_tag="hec"
      | fields _time collector_id signal_family metric_name mv exporter_tag lane ]
    [ search (index=otel OR index=observability OR index=main) sourcetype=otel:deadletter earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval collector_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(collector_id,host,pod,"dl_unk"))))
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats count AS mv BY _time collector_id
      | eval signal_family="deadletter"
      | eval metric_name="deadletter_record"
      | eval lane="deadletter_file"
      | eval exporter_tag="dl"
      | fields _time collector_id signal_family metric_name mv exporter_tag lane ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_ld latest(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_us FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
      | eval collector_id=lower(trim(Performance.host))
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval signal_family="host"
      | eval metric_name="perf_dm_anchor"
      | eval mv=coalesce(mem_us,0)
      | eval lane="tstats_perf"
      | eval exporter_tag="perf"
      | fields _time collector_id signal_family metric_name mv exporter_tag lane cpu_ld mem_us ]
| eval collector_id=coalesce(collector_id,"unknown_coll")
| eval exporter_tag=coalesce(exporter_tag,"__na__")
| bucket _time span=1m
| eval v_acc=if(match(metric_name,"receiver_accepted"),mv,0)
| eval v_sent=if(match(metric_name,"exporter_sent"),mv,0)
| eval v_sfail=if(match(metric_name,"send_failed"),mv,0)
| eval v_eqfail=if(match(metric_name,"enqueue_failed"),mv,0)
| eval v_pdrop=if(match(metric_name,"processor_dropped"),mv,0)
| eval v_pref=if(match(metric_name,"processor_refused"),mv,0)
| eval v_filt=if(match(metric_name,"filter_filtered"),mv,0)
| stats sum(v_acc) AS acc_sum sum(v_sent) AS sent_sum sum(v_sfail) AS sfail_sum sum(v_eqfail) AS eqfail_sum sum(v_pdrop) AS pdrop_sum sum(v_pref) AS pref_sum sum(v_filt) AS filt_sum max(eval(if(match(metric_name,"batch_batch_send"),mv,null()))) AS batch_span_hint max(cpu_ld) AS cpu_ld max(mem_us) AS mem_us values(lane) AS lanes BY _time collector_id exporter_tag
| sort 0 collector_id exporter_tag - _time
| streamstats window=5 global=f current=t first(acc_sum) AS a0 last(acc_sum) AS a1 first(sent_sum) AS s0 last(sent_sum) AS s1 first(sfail_sum) AS f0 last(sfail_sum) AS f1 BY collector_id exporter_tag
| eval acc_delta=round(a1-a0,4)
| eval sent_delta=round(s1-s0,4)
| eval sfail_delta=round(f1-f0,4)
| eval gap_ratio_pct=if(acc_delta>0.001, round(100*(acc_delta-sent_delta)/acc_delta,3), null())
| eventstats median(sfail_delta) AS med_sfail_delta BY collector_id exporter_tag
| eval severity=case(sfail_delta>10 OR (isnotnull(gap_ratio_pct) AND gap_ratio_pct>5),"critical", sfail_delta>0 OR eqfail_sum>0 OR (isnotnull(gap_ratio_pct) AND gap_ratio_pct>1) OR pdrop_sum>0 OR pref_sum>0 OR filt_sum>0,"high", true(),"low")
| eval loss_focus=case(sfail_delta>0 AND eqfail_sum>0,"exporter_retry_queue", sfail_delta>0,"exporter_hard_fail", pdrop_sum>0,"processor_drop", pref_sum>0,"processor_refuse", filt_sum>0,"filter_intent", true(),"observe")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","isolate_exporter_validate_tls_tokens_throttle_and_queue_caps", severity=="high","diff_config_replicas_audit_processors_feature_gates", true(),"baseline_review_after_change_window")
| join type=left max=0 collector_id
    [| inputlookup otel_collector_inventory.csv
     | fields collector_id env owner_team role image_tag otlp_endpoint_set ]
| eval env=coalesce(env,"unknown_env")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval otlp_endpoint_set=coalesce(otlp_endpoint_set,"unset_endpoints")
| table _time collector_id env owner_team exporter_tag otlp_endpoint_set lanes acc_delta sent_delta gap_ratio_pct sfail_delta pdrop_sum pref_sum filt_sum severity loss_focus recommended_action cpu_ld mem_us
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[observability_uc_13_3_14_otel_loss_forensics_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_3_14_otel_loss_forensics | where severity IN ("critical","high")

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above service level objectives, summarize each arm into a five-minute summary index keyed on collector_id and exporter_tag, widen alert searches to earliest=-6h on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on a representative collector pod. curl the internal /metrics endpoint and compare otelcol_exporter_send_failed_* counters to Splunk mstats rows for the same service_instance_id within one scrape interval. When numbers disagree, verify duplicate pod names across clusters, delayed metric ingestion, or relabel rules that strip labels before blaming parsers.

Validate accepted versus sent ratios cautiously: some builds omit otelcol_exporter_sent_* for certain exporters, which makes gap_ratio_pct null without implying innocence. When sent series are absent, lean on send_failed and enqueue_failed counters plus dead-letter lanes.

Log validation: kubectl logs for the same pod should mention exporter names, HTTP status codes, gRPC status details, or partial success language that aligns with Splunk otel:collector events. When logs disagree with metrics, suspect log sampling or truncated retention before clearing the alert.

Backend validation: reproduce a controlled 429 or 5xx response in a lab receiver and confirm send_failed counters increment while HEC corridor counts dip or dead-letter counts rise, depending on your failure handling.

Negative test: after a quiet window with healthy traffic, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for thirty consecutive runs unless a documented change is active.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Tail sampling decision cache exhaustion: tail_sampling processors can refuse or drop when decision caches fill; compare otelcol_processor_refused_* with tail_sampling logs, enlarge cache settings under vendor guidance, or reduce concurrent trace burstiness at ingress.

Case 2 — Batch processor backpressure: otelcol_processor_batch_batch_send_size spikes coupled with processor drops suggest oversized batches or slow exporters; tune send_batch_size and timeout, verify processor order, and ensure exporters keep pace before increasing batch limits.

Case 3 — OTLP exporter sender_queue saturated: enqueue_failed counters with stable send_failed may mean the sending queue cannot accept additional batches while retries drain; raise queue_size with disk-backed persistence only when security approves, and verify downstream ingest capacity.

Case 4 — Memory limiter hard drops at high water mark: processor drops appear alongside memory limiter language in logs while CPU remains moderate; lower soft limits carefully, reorder processors so limiters protect early, and scale replicas before raising heap pressure.

Case 5 — Dead-letter filelog exporter paths: dead-letter sourcetype rises while exporter send_failed falls after routing changes; validate file permissions, disk space, and retention on dead-letter volumes, then replay under change control.

Case 6 — Schema mismatch after feature gate or semantic convention flip: refuses cluster on one replica cohort after image upgrade; diff YAML between pods, align stabilizations, and roll forward or roll back as a unit.

Case 7 — mTLS handshake failure to backend: send_failed increments with handshake or certificate errors in logs; renew certificates, fix trust bundles, and validate SNI through corporate proxies before increasing retries.

Case 8 — Downstream throttle HTTP 429: send_failed or partial successes align with rate limit responses; negotiate quota, implement exponential backoff tuned to vendor guidance, and split traffic across multiple tokens or realms when appropriate.

Case 9 — Backend hard 5xx saturation: send_failed rises across all exporters sharing a region; engage backend owners, fail over if available, and reduce non-essential telemetry classes temporarily with explicit stakeholder approval.

Case 10 — Partial success on protobuf re-encode: logs mention partial success while metrics show mixed send outcomes; inspect per-signal error details, fix invalid spans or metric points at the source, and verify exporter translation settings.

Case 11 — Configuration drift between replicas: internal metrics show otelcol_processor_* only on a subset of service_instance_id values; reconcile DaemonSet generations, ConfigMap hashes, and cluster taints, then enforce GitOps convergence checks.

Closing checklist: confirm five em-dash step headers, Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly, multisearch lists five arms, coalesce streamstats eventstats case multisearch inputlookup join and tstats appear, and the closing table lists sixteen operational columns for routing and inventory cross-checks.

Teaching notes for new engineers: treat send_failed as a receipt that an exporter attempted delivery and the backend or transport rejected work, while enqueue_failed means the exporter could not even queue another batch. Together they distinguish retry pressure from immediate hard failure.

Governance: require paired change tickets when feature gates move, because stabilizations change wire formats faster than dashboards update.

Audit evidence: archive weekly CSV exports of critical and high severity rows with change identifiers and exclude raw payloads when policies require minimization.

Executive storytelling: translate gap_ratio_pct into probable customer impact using service maps maintained elsewhere; this search supplies mechanical evidence, not emotional priority.

Capacity partnership: finance reviewers appreciate charts that tie rising exporter failures to concrete backend throttle events rather than abstract noise budgets.

Security posture: restrict internal metrics scrape endpoints to observability namespaces and log scraper identities; labels can reveal service topology to insiders.

Mesh interactions: service meshes that inject mTLS may conflict with plaintext exporter endpoints; document intended TLS modes in inventory rows to avoid blind alleys during incidents.

IPv6-only environments: validate gRPC connectivity and DNS records independently of IPv4 assumptions; silent failures may appear only as intermittent send_failed bursts.

Windows versus Linux agents: parsing cost and threading models differ; when processor drops appear only on Windows hosts, investigate regex-heavy processors before blaming network paths.

Mainframe and appliance bridges: specialized forwarders may batch differently; confirm units on internal metrics before comparing to Linux gateway baselines.

Long-term improvement: when vendor distributions rename metrics, maintain a translation table in props.conf and version it beside collector images.

Post-incident prompts: ask whether dead-letter disks filled, whether tokens rotated without overlap, and whether partial rollouts left mixed feature gates across replicas.

Drills: twice per year, rehearse interpreting conflicting multisearch lanes so on-call staff explain why logs might show failures while metrics look quiet due to scrape loss.

Documentation hygiene: when collector_id keys change, backfill inventory lookups or accept brief breaks in longitudinal charts rather than silently remapping owners.

Stakeholder communications: product owners care about trace completeness; pair severity rows with sampling documentation so intentional statistical loss is not confused with mechanical drops.

Tooling: optional Observability Cloud navigators complement this search but do not replace internal metrics forensics when Splunk remains system of record.

Observability maturity: advance from reactive paging to proactive drift detection by trending median exporter failure rates per environment quarter over quarter.

Risk management: escalated retries can amplify outages; cap retry budgets during widespread backend failures unless leadership approves deliberate load.

Data minimization: dead-letter archives may contain sensitive attributes; encrypt at rest and purge on schedules aligned to privacy reviews.

Reliability partnership: after remediation, capture before-and-after internal metric snapshots in tickets to prove send_failed returned to zero and gap ratios normalized.

Architecture evolution: separate gateway pools for logs, metrics, and traces when noisy neighbors routinely collide; this search highlights which pipeline_family dominates failures.

Community processors: audit contrib processors for cardinality explosions that indirectly cause exporter failures through oversized payloads.

Clock discipline: skew between collectors and backends distorts correlation; monitor NTP health independently.

Proxy bodies: corporate proxies may enforce maximum body sizes smaller than collector batch limits; align limits with infrastructure partners.

Kafka exporters: broker instability may present as send_failed spikes; pair with broker health dashboards before retuning collectors alone.

Cloud egress: unexpected egress denials can surface as TLS errors; validate firewall change calendars against failure windows.

grandmaExplanation: We watch the paths that carry traces, metrics, and logs from programs into storage. When a checkpoint quietly throws packages away instead of slowing the line politely, we show which gate and which shipping lane broke so teams fix it before charts look fine while reality is thin.
hardware: Linux and Windows collector hosts, Kubernetes DaemonSet agents and gateway Deployments, Prometheus or scrape agents hitting /metrics, Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud receiving telemetry.
